Thiopental, characterized as having a rapid redistribution profile from the central compartment to peripheral tissues, accounts for its short duration of action. Upon administering a single dose, the drug diffuses out of the cerebrospinal fluid and enters muscles and fatty tissues, diluting the concentration in the brain and terminating the anesthetic effect sooner rather than later.
\boxed{A} Based on the evidence provided regarding thiopental’s pharmacokinetics, option \( A \): "Rapid biotransformation" does not directly explain why the effect terminates soon after a single dose.
